metric_trajectory.purpose: Track how the primary test metric for each SD has moved across substrate iterations. Enables predictive hypotheses: given the co-variation of metric value vs substrate state, what should the next experiment produce? The trajectory is more informative than any individual PASS/FAIL because it reveals whether the substrate is the bottleneck or the hypothesis.

fields.primary_metric: Name of the key metric this SD should move
fields.primary_metric_description: What it measures and why it is the right quantity
fields.direction: lower_is_better or higher_is_better -- which direction indicates substrate progress
fields.target: Threshold expression the fully-implemented SD must satisfy
fields.observations: Ordered list of experiments that have produced readings on this metric, annotated with substrate state at the time. Chronological order. Include non-contributory and superseded runs -- the zero-readings and failures are the most informative part of the trajectory.
fields.current_blocker: What is currently preventing the metric from reaching threshold, or null if the SD is implemented and validated
fields.prediction: Explicit forward hypothesis: given the current trajectory slope and substrate state, what value range should the next experiment produce, and under what conditions? If null, no confident prediction is possible yet.

observations.metric_notes: PASS all 5 seeds. z_harm_a representationally distinct from z_harm_s; D3 reversal absent. Nonredundancy criterion met. metric_trajectory.current_blocker: SD-022 substrate not active during EXQ-323. When z_harm_a derives from the same 50-dim proximity EMA as z_harm_s, the nonredundancy objective is applied to a pair of representations that share their information source. The training signal is therefore unstable: any reduction in cosine similarity achieved in one episode can be reversed when the proximity geometry changes. SD-022 (limb_damage_enabled=True) provides a causally independent information source for z_harm_a (body damage state), giving the nonredundancy objective a genuine basis for stable disentanglement.

prediction.expected_value: C1 passes 4+/5 seeds; cosine_sq_nonredundant < cosine_sq_baseline consistently; delta > 0.01
prediction.rationale: With SD-022 active, z_harm_a information source (body damage) is causally independent from z_harm_s information source (proximity field). The nonredundancy objective now works with genuinely separable representations. The co-variation across EXQ-241, EXQ-318, and EXQ-323 predicts that source independence is the rate-limiting factor for SD-019 consistency.
prediction.prerequisite_state: SD-022 limb_damage_enabled=True with harm_obs_a_variance > 0.001 (requires adequate hazard density or reduced heal_rate)
